mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, persistent near-zero policy rates compress the equity risk premium discount rate continuously, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors reach for yield regardless of month-to-month fluctuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX shows no return premium during zero-rate months versus non-zero-rate months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE stops rising and posts two consecutive monthly declines after a prolonged uptrend, this signals labor market inflection and future consumption recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price in cyclical earnings recovery.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines in 2 consecutive monthly readings after a prior 6-month uptrend
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% vintage_date 2009-10-01, near cycle peak
failure_condition: Following a confirmed two-month UNRATE decline signal, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month improvement in both HOUST and RSAFS indicates broad-based demand recovery across housing and retail sectors jointly, a combination that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month more reliably than either series alone because it confirms consumer and investment demand are recovering in tandem.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both post positive month-over-month growth simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 585 (revised) and RSAFS at 330998 (revised) as of Sept 2009 vintage, both off recession lows
failure_condition: Joint HOUST+RSAFS positive-growth signal fails to outperform single-series baseline models in Rank IC by more than 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During the early stage of a post-recession recovery, sustained month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects restocking and capacity utilization gains that translate into corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, but this relationship is specific to recovery regimes and weak in late-cycle expansions where industrial output growth no longer surprises markets.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value 87.4063 as of Sept 2009, rising from 2009 trough
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested specifically within NBER-dated recovery windows, or the effect reverses sign in late-cycle regimes without being flagged.

mechanism_narrative: The monthly change in PAYEMS acts as a continuously updated leading indicator of aggregate income and spending capacity; smaller job losses or emerging job gains are priced quickly into equities, producing a positive short-horizon relationship between PAYEMS momentum and S&P 500 5-day forward returns at all times.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value 130061 thousand as of Oct 2009, still declining but at a decelerating pace
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the full 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains low or subdued while FEDFUNDS sits near zero, the combination signals the central bank has room to maintain accommodative policy without inflation constraint, extending the equity-supportive liquidity regime and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; this mechanism fails once inflation crosses a threshold that would force policy tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change stays below 2% while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: When the joint low-inflation/zero-rate trigger is active, SPX 20-day forward returns underperform unconditional baseline in more than 50% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter in which GDPC1 growth turns positive while RSAFS simultaneously shows accelerating month-over-month growth confirms that both aggregate output and consumer spending are recovering together, an interaction that predicts sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter beyond what either series signals independently, since it confirms broad-based demand-side recovery rather than a narrow inventory bounce.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive concurrent with RSAFS 3-month average growth exceeding 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage showing recovery post-2009Q2 trough, RSAFS revised_value 330998 million rising from crisis lows
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1+RSAFS positive-growth signal does not outperform single-proxy GDPC1-only model by Rank IC margin > 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where UNRATE remains elevated near cycle highs but PAYEMS monthly job losses are shrinking, markets interpret this divergence as an early recovery signal specific to labor-market-trough regimes, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as traders front-run the eventual employment inflection; this mechanism is regime-specific and does not hold when UNRATE is low or stable.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE 10.0% revised (Oct 2009) near cycle high while PAYEMS monthly declines decelerating
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE above 8% and decelerating PAYEMS job losses, SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 in such regime-restricted samples.
